chapter_title: OF CERTAIN RELATIONS RESEMBLING THOSE CREATED BY CONTRACT
section_title: If any promisee neglects or refuses to afford the promisor reasonable
content:
67. If any promisee neglects or refuses to afford the promisor reasonable
neglect of
facilities for the performance of his promise, the promisor is excused by such
promisee to
afford neglect or refusal as to any non-performance caused thereby.

neglect or refusal.

chapter_title: OF CERTAIN RELATIONS RESEMBLING THOSE CREATED BY CONTRACT
section_title: If a person, incapable of entering into a contract, or any one whom he is
content:
68. If a person, incapable of entering into a contract, or any one whom he is
necessaries
legally bound to support, is supplied by another person with necessaries
supplied to
person suited to his condition in life, the person who has furnished such supplies is
incapable of
entitled to be reimbursed from the property of such incapable person.
contracting
or on his Illustrations
account
(a) A supplies B, a lunatic, with necessaries suitable to his condition in life. A
is entitled to be reimbursed from B's property.
(b) A supplies the wife and children of B, a lunatic, with necessaries suitable
to their condition in life. A is entitled to be reimbursed from B's property.

chapter_title: OF CERTAIN RELATIONS RESEMBLING THOSE CREATED BY CONTRACT
section_title: A person who is interested in the payment of money which another is
content:
69. A person who is interested in the payment of money which another is
of person
bound by law to pay, and who therefore pays it, is entitled to be reimbursed
paying
money due by the other.
by another
Illustration
in payment
of which he B holds land in Bangladesh, on a lease granted by A, the zamindar. The
is interested
revenue payable by A to the Government being in arrear, his lands is
advertised for sale by the Government. Under the
revenue law, the consequence of such sale will be the annulment of B's
lease. B, to prevent the sale and the consequent annulment of his own lease,
pays to the Government the sum due from A. A is bound to make good to B
the amount so paid.
Obligation

chapter_title: OF CERTAIN RELATIONS RESEMBLING THOSE CREATED BY CONTRACT
section_title: Where a person lawfully does anything for another person, or delivers
content:
70. Where a person lawfully does anything for another person, or delivers
of person
anything to him, not intending to do so gratuitously, and such other person
enjoying
benefit of enjoys the benefit thereof, the latter is bound to make compensation to the
non-
former in respect of, or to restore, the thing so done or delivered.
gratuitous
act
Illustrations
(a) A, a tradesman, leaves goods at B's house by mistake. B treats the
goods as his own. He is bound to pay A for them.
(b) A saves B's property from fire. A is not entitled to compensation from B, if
the circumstances show that he intended to act gratuitously.
Responsibility

sub_sections.text:
A, a tradesman, leaves goods at B's house by mistake. B treats the
goods as his own. He is bound to pay A for them.

A saves B's property from fire. A is not entitled to compensation from B, if
the circumstances show that he intended to act gratuitously.
